title: Change a Trash Bag
summary: Remove a full trash bag and replace it with a new one.
learnerPromise: You will be able to remove a full trash bag and install a new one so the bin is ready for use again.
whyItMatters: Knowing how to change a trash bag keeps bins from overflowing, reduces odors, and prevents pests. It is a quick task that makes a big difference in daily cleanliness.
realLifeUses: Emptying a full kitchen trash bag before it overflows
Replacing a bathroom trash bag after cleaning
Taking out trash before a trip so it does not sit and smell
Helping with chores in a shared home or workspace
result: A tied, removed trash bag and a bin lined with a fresh bag.
youWillLearn: How to gather and tie a full bag securely so nothing falls out
How to remove the bag without tearing it or spilling contents
How to place a new bag so it fits smoothly and stays in place
How to check for leaks or residue at the bottom of the bin
miniChallenge: Change the trash bag in one bin in your home today. Take the full bag to the outdoor trash if possible.
steps: Pull the edges of the full bag together and tie the top into a firm knot.
Lift the bag out carefully, supporting the bottom if it feels heavy.
Check the inside of the bin for leaks or residue and wipe it clean if needed.
Unfold a new trash bag and open it fully before placing it in the bin.
Fit the edges of the new bag over the rim of the bin so it stays secure.
completionCriteria: Full bag is tied shut and removed from the bin
New bag is fully open and secured around the bin rim
commonProblems: Bag tears while lifting — double-bag next time or take it out before it gets too heavy
New bag keeps slipping into the bin — tuck the edges firmly around the rim or use a bin with a built-in holder
Leak at the bottom — check for sharp objects and line the bin with newspaper next time
tips: Take the bag out before it is overflowing to make tying and carrying easier
Store extra bags at the bottom of the bin so they are always handy
If the bag is very heavy, ask someone to help carry it
